formula_note: 11 soldier deaths + 33 guerrilla deaths = 44 total combatant direct deaths.
uncertainty_note: This uses the clean categorical split from Colombia Aprende; other institutional sources disagree on overall totals.
quality_note: Publishable for this bounded site only because the source explicitly separates soldiers and guerrillas.

formula_note: Directly taken from the same categorical split used for pj_e1.
uncertainty_note: Disappeared civilians are excluded rather than recoded as confirmed direct deaths.
quality_note: Strong enough for the bounded-site DR, but still source-contingent.

formula_note: (35 M-19 attackers + 1,000 state troops) × (28 hours / 730.5 hours per month) = 39.67 person-months.
uncertainty_note: Likely conservative because an unquantified police special unit is mentioned in narrative sources but not added numerically.
quality_note: Strongest military exposure denominator in this wave because the theater is a single building and duration is short.

formula_note: 300 hostages/occupants × (28 hours / 730.5 hours per month) = 11.50 person-months.
uncertainty_note: Conservative because the source says more than 300 hostages; the estimate does not guess a larger figure.
quality_note: Building-bounded civilian exposure is the main reason a formal DR is defensible here.

formula_note: (43 / 11.50) ÷ (44 / 39.67) = 3.37, comparing combatant direct-death rate to civilian direct-death rate within the same site and time window. [corrected: formula was previously inverted (civ_rate/mil_rate); now uses standard mil_rate/civ_rate per definitions.md]
uncertainty_note: This should be read as a source-contingent bounded-site DR, not as a settled national-level fact about the entire Palace case.
quality_note: Publishable only because same-episode direct deaths and theater-specific denominators are both available, albeit imperfectly.
